key_insight: A coupled CEL finite-element model plus ANN surrogate can accurately predict temperatures, axial force, material flow, and weld defects in HDPE friction stir welding, enabling fast process optimization.
engineering_relevance: The study provides practical welding-window guidance for improving joint quality and reducing defects through parameter selection and bottom-surface preheating.
method: 3D Coupled Eulerian-Lagrangian (CEL) simulation of FSW under varying rotation speed, travel speed, and preheating
Two multilayer perceptron ANNs trained on experimental data to predict temporal temperature and axial-force responses
Process-map generation to identify defect-free and defective operating windows
evidence_signal: The ANN models achieved strong predictive performance with R2 values between 0.91 and 0.99, and the CEL model matched experimental observations of void formation and thermal/mechanical responses.
watch_item: The process map is based on the tested HDPE parameter space, so broader materials, tool geometries, or industrial conditions may require additional validation.
paper_title: Computational and machine learning modelling approaches for weld quality predictions in friction stir welding of high-density polyethylene
